text:
We present Video-LLaMA, a multi-modal framework designed to bridge the gap between Large Language Models (LLMs) and video understanding by enabling the processing of both visual and auditory signals. While existing efforts have successfully integrated either images or audio into LLMs, Video-LLaMA addresses the more complex challenge of comprehensive video comprehension, which requires capturing temporal dynamics and synchronizing audio-visual inputs.

To achieve this, we introduce two specialized components: a Vision-Language Branch and an Audio-Language Branch. The vision branch utilizes a Video Q-former to integrate temporal information with pre-trained image encoders, while the audio branch leverages ImageBind to map auditory signals into a common embedding space via an Audio Q-former. We employ a multi-stage, multi-branch cross-modal training strategy, first bootstrapping from large-scale video and image-caption datasets to establish alignment, followed by fine-tuning with high-quality instruction-following data. Our results demonstrate that Video-LLaMA effectively perceives and interprets video content, allowing it to generate meaningful, grounded responses to complex multi-modal queries. We have open-sourced the codebase and model weights to facilitate further research in audio-visual AI assistants.
